Stay where you are and we’ll send someone to find you. Return to Mainland BLOG Why Ginger and Turmeric Are Herbs of the Year Our two favorite herbs have each received well-deserved recognition. Both ginger and turmeric are being celebrated for their culinary significance, long history of traditional use and a growing body of modern scientific research. READ MORE Ginger Sourcing. Why Not Hawaii? Understanding the Realities of Commercial Production. When people think about the origin of ginger, Hawaii often comes to mind. With its lush climate and volcanic soil, it’s a place that seems ideal for growing tropical crops. But when it comes to commercial ginger production, Hawaii grows only a small amount of ginger compared to the rest of the world. READ MORE Boost Your GLP-1 Naturally: The Power of Ginger & Turmeric If you’re on GLP-1 medications or looking for natural ways to support your body’s GLP-1 levels, you’re not alone. But did you know that two common cooking spices you likely have in your kitchen right now —ginger and turmeric—could play a role in this journey? Let’s explore how these powerful spices may help. surfaces.title: Ultimate Chewy Ginger Snaps - The Ginger People US
surfaces.meta_description: We love our ginger snaps a little puffy, soft and chewy, with plenty of ginger and spice! Slightly more or less flour will make a thicker or thinner cookie – adjust the flour to suit your preference.
surfaces.text_excerpt: CHOOSE YOUR LOCATION: NORTH AMERICA EUROPE ASIA PACIFIC PRODUCTS BULK INGREDIENTS RECIPES ABOUT BLOG WHERE TO BUY RECIPES NEXT PRINT Pinterest Facebook X Email Write Review recipe note Using a scale makes this recipe move really fast. Make the dough ahead, roll into balls, coat with sugar and toss in the freezer. Pull them out as you need them. Bring to room temperature and bake. Recipe by House Chef, Abbie Leeson Learning through travel, Abbie has eaten her way through nearly 60 countries. Australia, Finland, Morocco, Japan and France - a few of her culinary favorites. She's passionate about taking ginger into unexpected places, while still revisiting the classics like her beloved Ultimate Chewy Ginger Snaps. Favorite culinary book: Salt, Fat, Acid, Heat. Favorite moment with a fellow chef: Dinner with Julia Child, 1992. Actually, she was at the next table. We told the waiter, "We'll have what she's having." Ultimate Chewy Ginger Snaps PREP: 10 MIN | BAKE: 8-10 MIN | CHILL: 1 HR | MAKES: 24 ♥ Staff Favorite – Winner of The Ginger People Cook-Off We love our cookies under baked and a little gooey, so we seldom go over the 8.5-minute mark in the oven. Try half dipping them in dark chocolate after they cool – unbelievable! We’ve also tried using Ghee in place of unsalted butter.
